message.content:
<deliberation>
Step-by-step plan:
1) Parse the main_entry_nodes and map them to the provided main_nodes text.
2) Identify explicit cross-references in each main node and match them to referenced_nodes provided.
3) Read the target policy chunk and compare it to each main node (plus its cross-references), looking for contradictions or alterations. Treat omissions as Non-Deviation per Rule 4.
4) Classify any deviation strictly using the provided taxonomy.
5) For each main node, prepare an evaluation object including reg_id, short clause name, considered_references, deviation_found flag, and deviations array.

Evaluations:
- REG-017 (Art14(2)):
  • Cross-refs: Art6(1) incl. point (f) (REG-001); Art9(2) incl. point (a) (REG-009); Art22(1) (REG-041); Art22(4) not provided.
  • Policy chunk only mentions the Art 77 complaint right; it does not discuss any Art14(2) disclosures. These are omissions and must be treated as Non-Deviation. No conflicting statement is present.

- REG-021 (Art15(1)):
  • Cross-refs: Art22(1) and (4); only Art22(1) provided (REG-041).
  • Policy chunk is silent on access rights scope/details; this is omission and thus Non-Deviation. No conflict.

- REG-092 (Art77(1)):
  • No explicit cross-refs inside 77(1).
  • Policy says: “you have the right to lodge a complaint … if you believe processing infringes data protection law.” GDPR text says “… if the data subject considers … infringes this Regulation” and specifies “in particular” Member State venues. Omissions are Non-Deviation. However, replacing “this Regulation” with broader “data protection law” is a change in the triggering condition’s legal scope/phrasing. Classify as Execution style deviation (phrasing altering the legal reference), noting it may be broader than GDPR but is not necessarily non-compliant.

- REG-093 (Art77(2)):
  • Cross-ref: Art78 (REG-094, REG-095, REG-096, REG-097).
  • Policy chunk does not cover supervisory authority informing complainant on progress/outcome or about judicial remedy. Omission => Non-Deviation. No contradictory statement.

- REG-095 (Art78(2)):
  • Cross-refs in text: Arts 55, 56 (not provided), and it is pursuant to Art77; also broader context in Art78(1), (3), (4) (REG-094, REG-096, REG-097). Consider 77(1)-(2) (REG-092, REG-093) for context.
  • Policy chunk is silent on judicial remedies/time-limit (three months). Omission => Non-Deviation. No conflict.

Output JSON will include one object per main node with considered references and deviations accordingly.
</deliberation>
```json
[
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-017",
    "clause": "Art14(2)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-001", "REG-009", "REG-041"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-021",
    "clause": "Art15(1)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-041"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-092",
    "clause": "Art77(1)",
    "considered_references": [],
    "deviation_found": true,
    "deviations": [
      {
        "deviation_type": "Execution style deviation",
        "analysis": "Art77(1) grants a right to lodge a complaint if the data subject considers that processing infringes 'this Regulation' and notes venues 'in particular' in the Member State of habitual residence, place of work, or place of the alleged infringement. The policy states the right exists if processing infringes 'data protection law' and does not restate the venue detail. Omissions are treated as non-deviations, but substituting 'this Regulation' with the broader term 'data protection law' alters the phrasing and legal reference compared to Art77(1)."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-093",
    "clause": "Art77(2)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-094", "REG-095", "REG-096", "REG-097"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-095",
    "clause": "Art78(2)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-092", "REG-093", "REG-094", "REG-096", "REG-097"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  }
]
```
